evidence_excerpt: The fistula was successfully treated and the patient was hemodynamically stabilized; however, the patient died of pneumonia and sepsis two months after the procedure.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Initial massive bleeding required TEVAR; despite immediate stabilization, he developed pneumonia and sepsis and died two months later, indicating severe illness during follow-up.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: After TEVAR and left subclavian artery coiling, the fistula was successfully treated and the patient was hemodynamically stabilized.
Two months after the procedure, the patient developed pneumonia and sepsis.
The patient died of pneumonia and sepsis two months after the procedure.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact date of diagnosis was not provided; the index time was taken as the second hospital visit when CT/angiography confirmed ALSA–esophageal fistula and treatment (TEVAR/coiling) was planned. Functional status after treatment was not explicitly described; severe impairment was inferred from the persistent bed-ridden baseline and absence of documented improvement before death.
